provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This Scheme may be cited as the Civil Service Scheme , 2005 and shall come into operation on the date of publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: This Scheme shall apply to and in respect of all civil servants.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In this Scheme unless the context requires otherwise— “ Act ” means the Public Service Act; [ Act No.8 of 2002 ] “ appellate authority ” means a body or organ or a person empowered by the Act to decide upon civil servants’ appeals; “ appellate authority ” means a body or organ or a person empowered; “ appointments ” means recruitment of persons in the Civil Service and shall include first appointments or appointments on promotion; “ appointing authority ” means a body or organ or person empowered by the Act to make appointments of public servants in the Civil Service; “ approved Establishments ” means the establishment of posts in the Civil Service which there is provision in the approved budget for their establishment; “ authorised person ” means a civil servant who is authorised in writing in his organisation either generally or specifically to act on matters of any specific kind or to perform any duty or any responsibility; “ Chairman ” means a person who has been appointed to the post of Chairman of the Commission , the Chairman of the Independent Department and any other person acting in the office of the Chairman; “ Chief Executive Officers ” means the Permanent Secretary, Deputy Permanent Secretary, Head of Independent Department, Regional Administrative Secretary, Director of Local Government Authority , Clerk of the National Assembly, Director of Immigration Service and Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services; “ Chief Secretary ” means a Public Servant appointed by the President under section 4 of the Act to assist the President on the administration of the public service ; “ civil service ” is the executive and administrative body of public servants employed by the Government other than those employed in the local government service, teachers service, health service, fire and rescue and immigration service; “ civil service post ” means a post in the civil service ; “ Civil Service Scheme ” means a government administrative legal document or rules stipulating a formal or official order or way of doing things for the purpose of enabling civil servants to serve the public in an orderly and accountable manner; “ civil servant ” means a person holding or acting in the civil service office; “Code of Ethics and Conducts” means the Code of Ethics and Conducts applicable to civil servants; “ Commission ” means the Public Service Commission established by the Act ; “ criminal offence ” means an offence chargeable in the criminal court as defined in the Criminal Procedures Act , 1985; [ Act No.9 of 1985 ] “ department or division ” means an identified entity within the organisational structure for management purposes; “ disciplinary authority ” means any person vested with powers by the Act to take disciplinary measures against any public servant and includes any person to whom those powers have been delegated; “ disciplinary offence ” means an offence which is specified in the Act as warranting disciplinary proceedings; “ employer ” means a person or organisation, in the civil service with whom a civil servant entered into a contract of service and who is responsible for the payment of salaries of such a civil servant ; “ employee ” means a person employed in the civil service ; “ independent department ” means a department of the Government which is not under the direct control of the Ministry; “ Local Government Authority ” means the Local Government Authority as defined in the Act ; “ Minister ” means the Minister for the time being responsible for the public service ; “ public service ” means the system or organisation entrusted with the, responsibility of overseeing the provision or directly providing the general public with what they need from the government as permissible by laws; and include the service in: (a) the service in the civil service ; (b) the teachers service; (c) the local government service; (d) the health service; (e) the immigration and the fire and rescue service; (f) the executive agencies; and (g) the public institutions services; and (h) the operational service . “ operational service ” means the cadre of supporting staff not employed in the executive or officer grade; “ public service post ” means a post in the public service ; “ public servant ” means a personal holding or acting in the public service office; “ renitent ” means the process aimed to get a new Employee to the civil service ; “ The service ” means the public service of the United Republic of Tanzania. Part II – Administration of the service

provisions.title: Powers of the President
provisions.content: (1) All executive functions of the civil service shall be performed by civil servants on behalf of the President. (2) All authorities in the civil service vested with powers under the Act shall exercise those powers on behalf of the President and shall act with obedience abiding to the directives issued in that respect.

provisions.title: The Minister
provisions.content: (1) The Minister shall be responsible for the public service as well as the civil service . (2) The Minister shall be a leader and overseer in the implementation of this Scheme and policies governing the Civil Service. (3) Every Minister in the Public Service shall ensure effective implementation of this Scheme in respect of civil servants within his Ministry.

provisions.title: Powers of the Chief Secretary
provisions.content: (1) The Chief Secretary as the Head and the Chief Executive Officer of the public service shall have the overall executive powers and shall be the highest ranking disciplinary authority to the civil service . (2) The Chief Secretary may exercise all or any of the powers delegated to a disciplinary authority in relation to any civil servant .

provisions.title: The office of Public Service Management
provisions.content: The Office of Public Service Management shall be the central organisation responsible for the management and administration through which the Government exercises control of the civil service and ensure that it is in good shape to serve as an efficient and effective machinery for executing the policies of the Government.

provisions.title: Powers of the Permanent Secretary (Establishments)
provisions.content: (1) The Permanent Secretary (Establishments) shall be the head and the chief executive officer of the civil service and after consultation with the Minister shall: (a) conduct investigations and studies concerning development and improvement of the efficiency of civil servants and shall take appropriate steps to ensure the attainment of civil service efficiency; (b) coordinate and ensure proper upkeep of personnel information for civil servants; (c) facilitate labour mobility of civil servants among employers; (d) constitute and abolish civil service offices except the office of Permanent Secretary and Deputy Permanent Secretary; (e) formulate, promulgate, monitor, evaluate, review and interpret administrative and personnel policies for the civil service ; (f) notify the Chief Secretary of vacancies in the civil service offices in respect of which the President is the appointing authority ; (g) ensure that all appointments in the Civil Service are in accordance with the approved establishments; (h) perform any other duties and functions assigned to him by the Minister responsible for civil service and the Chief Secretary ; (2) Powers of the Permanent Secretary (Establishments) under sub-clause (1) of this clause shall not be construed to affect the powers of other Permanent Secretaries in respect of civil servants within their jurisdictions.

provisions.title: Powers of Chief Executives Officer in the civil service
provisions.content: (1) Every Chief Executive Officer shall have powers and be the authority for appointments , confinnation of appointments and promotions of civil servants in his Ministry, Independent department or Region. (2) Every Chief Executive Officer shall have disciplinary powers and be the highest ranking disciplinary authority of civil servants in his Ministry, Independent Department or Region including civil servants in the operational service . (3) In exercising his powers the Chief Executive Officer shall observe and adhere to rules and procedures governing the civil service .

provisions.title: Powers of head of department, division or unit
provisions.content: (1) The head of department, division or unit shall be the disciplinary authority in respect of civil servants in the operational service . (2) When exercising his disciplinary powers the head of department, division or unit shall observe rules and procedures governing the civil service . (3) The head of department, division or unit shall have powers of supervision of all civil servants under him and shall ensure the availability of conducive and friendly working environment.

provisions.title: Powers of head of section
provisions.content: The head of section shall have powers of supervision of civil servants under him and shall give directives and ensure proper performance of the civil service activities in his respective section.

provisions.title: Civil servants in the operational service
provisions.content: (1) The powers provided for in clause 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8 of this Scheme shall be applicable to civil servants in the operational service except for disciplinary powers which shall be as provided for in clause 10 of this Scheme. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-Clause (1) of this clause, powers of the Chief Secretary provided for in clause 6 of this Scheme shall be applicable to the civil servants in operational service .

provisions.title: Civil Service Department of the Public Service Commission
provisions.content: The Civil Service Department of the Public Service Commission shall be responsible in matters related to the civil service and shall have the following powers— (a) to perform ground work for the Commission to facilitate merit based recruitment; (b) to receive, scrutinise, prepare summaries of civil servants appeals and submit to the Commission for decision; (c) to prepare grounds for the Commission to facilitate, monitor and evaluate performance by officials in the civil service to ensure results oriented management; (d) to perform any other duty as shall be assigned to it by the Commission in relation to the civil service . Part III – The Service

provisions.title: The role of the Civil Service
provisions.content: (1) The constitutional and practical role of the Civil Service is to assist honestly, impartially and with integrity duly constituted Government of the United Republic of Tanzania, whatever its political complexion, in formulating their policies, carrying out decisions and in administering Public Services for which it is responsible. (2) Subject to the-provisions of this Scheme, civil servants owe their loyalty to the Government in which they serve.

provisions.title: Civil servants’ duty to serve the Government
provisions.content: Every civil servant shall serve his Government in accordance with the principles set out in this Scheme and recognising— (a) the accountability of civil servants to the leaders and to their supervisors; (b) the duty of all public servants to discharge public functions reasonably and according to the law; (c) the duty to comply with the law and to uphold the administration of justice; and (d) ethical standards governing particular professions.

provisions.title: Terms and conditions of service
provisions.content: (1) The terms and conditions of the civil service shall be— (a) permanent and pensionable; (b) agreement or contract; (c) temporary month to month; (d) operational service ; (e) daily paid. (2) The terms and conditions of the civil service provided for under sub-clause (1) of this clause may be modified by any special terms contained in the agreements, contracts or letters of appointment of individual civil servant .

provisions.title: Appointments in the Civil Service
provisions.content: Appointments in the Civil Service shall be governed by the Constitution, the Act , the Public Service Regulations, the Public Service Recruitment Code of Practice, the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct, Public Service professional codes of Ethics and Conduct and Administrative Instructions or Orders.

provisions.title: Qualifications for appointment and promotion
provisions.content: No person shall be appointed to the civil service or promoted unless he meets the following necessary requirements— (a) academic qualification determined by a certain level of education required in the post concerned as indicated in the relevant Scheme of Service; (b) professional competency determined by the skill level of that profession; (c) age qualification determined by date of birth shown either in a birth certificate or any other document as may be determined by the head of the public service ; (d) good character and integrity by his appearance, interaction with other people, the manner he conducts himself, the manner he expresses himself and his track record; (e) experience determined by length of time he has served in the same post or equivalent post; (f) citizenship determined by birth certificate or registration certificate; (g) any other qualifications as may be prescribed in that post.

provisions.title: Notification of vacancies
provisions.content: (1) The appointing authorities in the civil service shall be obliged to notify the Commission on vacancies within their respective organisations. (2) Notification of vacancies shall specify the necessary requirements for the vacant posts and shall follow the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations and Administrative directives or instructions or orders.

provisions.title: Persons not to be appointed in the civil service
provisions.content: (1) No person who has been convicted of a criminal offence involving moral turpitude or who has been dismissed from the public service previously, shall be appointed to the civil service without prior sanction of the Chief Secretary . (2) Offences involving moral turpitude shall include— (a) an act or behaviour that gravely violates the moral sentiment or accepted moral standard of the community; (b) the immorally culpable quality held to be inherent in some criminal offences as distinguished from others. (3) No person who has been removed or retired in public interest or retired by retrenchment or redundancy from the Public Service, shall be appointed to the civil service without prior sanction of the Chief Secretary . (4) A person who is retired by retrenchment or redundancy from the public service shall, after the sanction of the Chief Secretary , be reappointed to the civil service on contract terms. (5) No person who is of unsound mind, shall be appointed the civil.

provisions.title: Approved establishment
provisions.content: (1) No appointment shall be made in the civil service , whether temporary or otherwise, for which no provision has been made in the estimates. (2) No increase in the number of posts may be made above the authorised establishment without prior approval of the Permanent Secretary (Establishments).

provisions.title: Responsibility of appointing authorities
provisions.content: The ultimate responsibility for appointments of the civil Servants, shall rest upon the appointing authorities.

provisions.title: Appointment principles
provisions.content: (1) All appointments in the civil service shall be governed by the overriding principles of selection based on merit, the well informed choice of individuals who through their abilities, experience and qualities match the need of the vacant post concerned. (2) The principles of selection for appointments of civil servants shall I be as provided for in the Public Service Recruitment Code of Practice. (3) The appointment procedures shall be subject to the principles equality and shall be appropriate for the nature of the post, the size and weight of responsibility. (4) Appointments in the civil service shall be free from religious beliefs, tribalism, gender biasness, regionalism and political partiality and shall be based on: (a) merit, fairness and open competition; (b) the prescribed qualifications.

provisions.title: Complaints regarding appointments or recruitments
provisions.content: (1) Where a candidate is dissatisfied with the way appointment procedure is conducted to any post which he is interested and which he was a candidate, he shall complain to the appointing authority concerned and if not satisfied with the decision of the appointing authority he shall appeal to the Commission and thereafter to the Chief Secretary . (2) The recruitment competition process shall be fully documented and the document shall be kept for record purposes for a period of not less than twelve months from the date when such recruitment process ended.

provisions.title: Constitution and abolition of posts
provisions.content: (1) The Permanent Secretary (Establishments) shall exercise powers vested in him by section 8(2)(d) of the Act to constitute, abolish, upgrade or downgrade civil service posts other than the post of the Chief Secretary , the Permanent Secretary and Deputy Permanent Secretary. (2) For the purposes of the provisions of sub-clause (1) of this clause, the words “upgrading” and “downgrading” of posts shall mean change in salary scale or entry point. (3) The powers under sub-clause (1) of this clause may be exercised in the same manner as provided for in sub-clauses (3), (4), (5) and (6) of clause 38 of the Public Service Scheme in respect of civil servants. (4) In exercise of his powers in sub-clause (1) The Permanent Secretary (Establishments) shall publish civil service post constituted, abolished, upgraded or down graded.

provisions.title: Termination of appointments
provisions.content: (1) Termination of appointments of civil servants shall include— (a) removal or retirement in public interest; (b) retirement on medical grounds; (c) retirement on abolition of office or re-organisation of department; (d) termination by age of retirement; (e) termination upon contesting constitutional leadership or elective political post; (f) retrenchment or redundancy by reason of: (i) abolition of office; (ii) re-organisation of office; (iii) resolution of the organisation; (iv) employer ’s incapability to remunerate a public servant ; (g) termination on disciplinary grounds. (2) Procedures for termination of appointments shall be as provided for in the Public Service Regulations. (3) Suggestions for removal or retirement in public interest shall be made by the Chief Executive Officer himself in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations ands shall only be made where the Chief Executive Officer is of the opinion that grounds for such suggestion do not warrant disciplinary actions. [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ]

provisions.title: Civil servants accountability
provisions.content: (1) The accountability in the civil service shall be hierarchical and civil servants shall be accountable according to arrangements from lower to higher ranks and shall be based on the hierarchy of authority and responsibility. (2) In the higher rank there shall be the Permanent Secretaries, Heads of Independent Departments and Regional Administrative Secretaries who are the Chief Executives according to section 5(1)(a) of the Act . (3) Below the Chief Executives there shall be the Heads of Departments or Divisions followed by the Heads of Sections then by the supporting administrative professional and technical staff comprising senior, middle and junior level civil servants. (4) Every Chief Executive Officer shall be accountable for the action of all civil servants in his jurisdiction. (5) The most senior civil servant is accountable for the actions of all civil servants below him and every civil servant in-charge of any specific area such as a supervisor, a scheduled officer and an auxiliary officer shall be accountable for the efficient performance in his area of responsibility. (6) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-clause (1), (2), (3), (4), and (5) of this clause, every civil servant shall be accountable for his own performance and actions provided that no interference, undue influence or pressure is exerted upon him by his senior or any public leader.

provisions.title: Relationship between a civil servant and his supervisor
provisions.content: (1) Every civil servant like any public servant shall be loyal to the President of the United Republic of Tanzania and shall perform his duties with regard to the requirements of the Constitution, Laws, Regulations, Orders, Directives and Instructions. (2) In performing duties every civil servant shall comply with lawful instructions of his supervisor; strive to serve loyally; respect and co-operate with his fellow public servants at work place and elsewhere within the public service in order to achieve the objective of the public service . (3) Every civil servant is entitled to recognition and respect for his dignity regardless of hierarchy in the civil service . (4) Subject to the laid down procedures a civil servant shall have right to claim and enquire on any issue pertaining to his service and his employer or supervisor shall grant the right claimed or avail him with the appropriate or necessary information enquired.

provisions.title: Relationship between a political leader and a civil servant
provisions.content: (1) The relationship between a political leader and a civil servant while performing their duties and functions, shall be governed by the Constitution and the relevant laws conferring powers on them. (2) Matters of leadership shall be under the powers of the political leader while matters of administration shall be under the powers of the executive officer incharge. (3) There shall not be any political interference with the civil servant while performing his executive duties and functions and where necessary it shall be through the appropriate supervisors.

provisions.title: Communication within the Civil Service
provisions.content: The method of communication within the Civil Service shall be as provided for in Part X of the Public Service Regulations.

provisions.title: The civil service working documents
provisions.content: (1) The necessary civil service working documents shall include— (a) the Constitution of the United Republic of Tanzania; (b) the Public Service Act ; [ Act No. 8 of 2002 ] (c) the Public Service Retirement Benefits Act ; [ Act No.2 of 1999 ] (d) the Political Leaders Retirement Benefits Act ; [ Act No.3 of 1999 ] (e) the Public Service (Negotiating Machineiy) Act ; [ Act No. 19 of 2003 ] (f) the Employment and Labour Relations Act ; [ Act No.6 of 2004 ] (g) the Executive Agencies Act ; [ Act No.30 of 1997 ] (h) the Regional Administration Act ; [ Act No. 19 of 1997 ] (i) the Labour Institutions Act ; [ Act No.7 of 2004 ] (j) the Public Service Regulations, 2003; [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ] (k) the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct; (l) various professional Codes of Ethics and Conduct; (m) Standing Orders for the Public Service; (n) the Public Service Recruitment Code of practice; (o) the Public Service Disciplinary Code of practice; (p) the Public Service Arbitration and Reconciliation Code of practice; (q) the Public Service Retirement and Redundancy Code of Practice; (r) the Public Service Management and Employment Policy; (s) any other laws and documents relevant for reference in relation to specific matters. (2) Every employer shall ensure the availability and accessibility of all necessary public service working documents for civil servants Under him. (3) Every civil servant shall make sure that he has access to all necessary public service working documents, he understands them and makes use of them. (4) Every civil servant shall make correct interpretation and reference of the working documents in order to reach proper decisions. (5) Where a civil servant fails to interpret or wants to confirm the interpretation of a provision of any law or any document, he shall seek assistance of any government legal expert available in the area.

provisions.title: Performance appraisal system
provisions.content: There shall be operated an open performance appraisal system by every organisation within the Civil Service in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations.

provisions.title: Performance contracts
provisions.content: Every civil servant shall sign a performance contract with his supervisor in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations.

provisions.title: Training and staff development
provisions.content: (1) Every employer shall be responsible for training and development of his staff for the purposes of developing individual skills for the performance of their present duties or future jobs. (2) Parent Ministry where necessary shall facilitate training and staff development of civil servants.

provisions.title: Transfers
provisions.content: (1) Where necessary and where it is in the public interest, transfers of civil servants shall be conducted within the public service for the purposes of improving efficiency of the public service . (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-clause (1) of this clause a civil servant shall not be transferred from one service to another with different terms and conditions of service without his consent. (3) Where a civil servant is transferred he shall be entitled to free transport for himself, spouse and four children or dependants as well as his personal belongings.

provisions.title: Medical services
provisions.content: (1) All civil servants serving in whatever terms shall be entitled to medical attention governed by National Social Security Act , National Health Insurance, or any other scheme. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-clause (1) of this clause, every civil servant shall take all reasonable precautions in accordance with modern health practices for the proper protection of his heath.

provisions.title: Employers duty to protect the health of civil servants
provisions.content: (1) Every employer shall take all reasonable precautions to ensure occupational safety standards in order to avoid unnecessary causes of health hazards and shall facilitate annual medical check up for every civil servant in his jurisdiction. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-clause (1) of this clause every civil servant shall have a duty to protect himself from any health hazards.

provisions.title: Leave
provisions.content: (1) Every civil servant shall be entitled to annual leave which shall be granted by the employer or supervisor once a year in accordance with the civil servant ’s leave cycle. (2) Non-grant of the civil servant ’s annual leave by the employer shall entitle such a civil servant a one month salary payment in lieu thereof. (3) There shall be other leave entitlements for the public servant in accordance with the provisions of the Public Service Regulations.

provisions.title: Compensation for injuries or death
provisions.content: Where a civil servant sustains injuries or dies in the cause of his employment, he shall be entitled to compensation which shall be paid in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations.

provisions.title: Civil servants representation
provisions.content: Civil servants shall be represented in the discussions with their employers on matters of employment welfare in general, through the workers’ councils and the Joint Staff Council.

provisions.title: Grievances handling mechanism
provisions.content: It shall be the role of every supervisor and every chief executive officer to establish a grievances handling mechanism and he shall receive, solve or seek solutions for the grievances of civil servants under his jurisdiction. Part IV – Discipline A – General

provisions.title: Conduct of civil servants
provisions.content: (1) Every civil servant shall conduct himself with integrity, impartiality and honesty. He shall give honest and impartial advice to the President or, as the case may be, to the Minister , to the Chief Executive or to the Head of his Department or to his Supervisor without fear or favour and shall make all information relevant for decision available to them. (2) A civil servant shall not deceive or knowingly mislead the President, Minister , Parliament, National Assembly, his Head of Department, his Supervisor or any member of the Public. (3) Every civil servant shall not conduct himself in a way which— (a) is illegal; (b) is improper; (c) is unethical; (d) is in breach of Constitutional Convention or a Professional Code of Ethics and Conduct; (e) is inconsistent with the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct and the Public Service Leadership Code of Ethics and Conduct; (f) involves possible maladministration. (4) A civil servant shall conduct himself in accordance with the provisions of: (a) the Public Service Codes of Ethics and Conduct; (b) the Public Service Leadership Code of Ethics and Conduct; (c) various Professional Codes of Ethics and Conduct; (d) the Public Service Regulations, the Administrative Directives or Instructions and Orders. (5) A civil servant shall endeavour to deal with the affairs of the public sympathetically, efficiently, promptly and without bias or maladministration. (6) A civil servant shall display an attitude of responsibility to the public property and money and shall endeavour to ensure the proper, effective and efficient use of them. (7) A civil servant shall not misuse his official position or information acquired in the course of his official duties to further his private interests or those of others. He shall not receive benefits of any kind from a third party which might reasonably be seen to compromise his personal judgement or integrity. (8) A civil servant shall not seek to frustrate the policies, decisions or actions of Government. (9) Every civil servant who is aware of any breaches or who has been required by any other public servant , whether senior or junior to act in a way which raises a fundamental issue of conscience for that civil servant , may report that breach to his supervisor or his employer as the case may be. (10) Any civil servant who contravenes provisions of this clause shall commit a disciplinary offence and shall be subject to disciplinary proceedings which shall be conducted in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations.

provisions.title: Civil servants integrity and behaviour
provisions.content: (1) Every civil servant shall serve with high standard of wisdom and integrity. (2) A civil servant shall behave as a servant not as a master and shall show courteous.

provisions.title: Confidentiality
provisions.content: A civil servant shall not without authority disclose official information which has been communicated confidentially within the administration or received confidentially from other public servants. 4

provisions.title: Political leader’s intervention
provisions.content: (1) A civil servant shall not seek any political leader’s intervention when pursuing matters of his service unless he proves that he has exhausted the executive administrative ladder. (2) Where a civil servant pursues any matter concerning his service, he shall do so by starting from his immediate supervisor before going to the higher level of the Civil Service or public service administration, provided that he shall not be bound to start from his immediate supervisor where he thinks that justice shall not be done. (3) Where in any way, a civil servant seeks any political leader’s intervention, a political leader whose intervention has been sought, shall, before intervention, communicate the matter to the chief executive officer of that civil servant . B – Disciplinary proceedings

provisions.title: Disciplinary authorities
provisions.content: The disciplinary authorities of civil servants shall be as provided for in the Public Service Regulations.

provisions.title: Preliminary investigations
provisions.content: Where the disciplinary authority considers necessary to institute disciplinary proceedings against a civil servant , the disciplinary authority shall make preliminary investigations before instituting disciplinary proceedings.

provisions.title: Appeals
provisions.content: (1) The Appellate Authorities of civil servants and the procedures for appeals shall be as provided for in the Public Service Regulations. (2) Both the employer or the disciplinary authority and a civil servant shall have the right to appeal if aggrieved with any decision. Part V – Retirement benefits

provisions.title: Grant of retirement benefits
provisions.content: (1) Retirement benefits of civil servants shall be granted in accordance with the following terms and conditions of service— (a) Permanent and pensionable; (b) Non-pensionable; (c) Contract or Agreement. (2) A civil servant employed on permanent and pensionable terms of service shall be granted a pension or gratuity as a right for the service he has rendered to the Government. (3) Where a civil servant does not qualify for pension he. shall be granted gratuity. (4) A civil servant in the non-pensionable terms of service shall be granted gratuity on termination of his service if he qualifies in accordance with the requirements of the retirement benefits laws relevant at that time. (5) A civil servant in the contract terms of service shall be granted gratuity on termination of his service if he qualifies in accordance with the terms of contract he entered.

provisions.title: Qualifications for terminal benefits
provisions.content: Qualifications for terminal benefits for civil servants shall be as provided for in various relevant terminal benefits laws.

provisions.title: Terminal benefits schemes
provisions.content: The terminal benefits schemes available for civil servants are contributory under the following funds:— (a) Public Service Pensions Fund (PSPF); (b) National Social Security Fund (NSSF); (c) Government Employees Provident Fund (GEPF).

provisions.title: Age of retirement
provisions.content: (1) Age of retirement for civil servants shall be as provided for in the retirement benefits laws applicable for the time being. (2) Where there is ambiguity on the date of birth of a civil servant and where there is argument between the employer and a civil servant as to the accurate date of birth, the date on record disclosed by a civil servant during his first appointment shall be recognised as the accurate date of birth of such civil servant . (3) Where only the year and not the actual date of birth of a civil servant has been recorded, his date of birth shall be deemed to be the first day of July of that year. (4) Where only the month of birth has been recorded, the date of birth shall be deemed to be the sixteenth day of that month and the fifteenth in the case of February.

provisions.title: Duty to keep records
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of both employer and employee to keep employment records for the purposes of reference for calculation of the employee ’s terminal benefits. (2) Employment records kept by both employer and employee shall be regarded as original copies duly signed by authorised person . (3) Where there is no possibility to get hold of employment records from the employer ’s end, records to custody of the employee shall be used for any purpose provided that such records are in the original form. definitions.definition: the Public Service Act
a body or organ or a person empowered by the Act to decide upon civil servants’ appeals
recruitment of persons in the Civil Service and shall include first appointments or appointments on promotion
a body or organ or person empowered by the Act to make appointments of public servants in the Civil Service
the establishment of posts in the Civil Service which there is provision in the approved budget for their establishment
a civil servant who is authorised in writing in his organisation either generally or specifically to act on matters of any specific kind or to perform any duty or any responsibility
a person who has been appointed to the post of Chairman of the Commission , the Chairman of the Independent Department and any other person acting in the office of the Chairman

the Permanent Secretary, Deputy Permanent Secretary, Head of Independent Department, Regional Administrative Secretary, Director of Local Government Authority , Clerk of the National Assembly, Director of Immigration Service and Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services
a Public Servant appointed by the President under section 4 of the Act to assist the President on the administration of the public service
a post in the civil service

a government administrative legal document or rules stipulating a formal or official order or way of doing things for the purpose of enabling civil servants to serve the public in an orderly and accountable manner
a person holding or acting in the civil service office
the Code of Ethics and Conducts applicable to civil servants
the Public Service Commission established by the Act
an offence chargeable in the criminal court as defined in the Criminal Procedures Act , 1985
an identified entity within the organisational structure for management purposes
any person vested with powers by the Act to take disciplinary measures against any public servant and includes any person to whom those powers have been delegated
an offence which is specified in the Act as warranting disciplinary proceedings
a person or organisation, in the civil service with whom a civil servant entered into a contract of service and who is responsible for the payment of salaries of such a civil servant
a person employed in the civil service
a department of the Government which is not under the direct control of the Ministry
